blocks.content: The true origin story of Objet is never clearly known.
Only the name of the item was passed down from someone, and through that, people let their imaginations soar.
However, the Last Wail of the Yeager Family is unique in that an actual record remains.
The merchant who acquired the mansion soon realized what powers the 287 beads possessed.
If one possessed it, a warning from a girl would be heard the moment they encountered a murderous intent.
It was such a sharp alarm device that even the most skilled and restrained murderous intent would not be able to avoid it.
They say the merchant sold it at auction and became the continent's wealthiest person.
And the 287 Objets passed into the hands of rulers of various kingdom, with currently about 82 estimated to remain.

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: Indeed.
This was the story Zenogre heard during his assassination training.
And that made the current situation all the more bizarre.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where… did you get that? You don't seem to have a life worth such a price.
Shirone scrambled to his feet, taking up a fighting stance.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What are you? What were you trying to do to me?
Without answering, Zenogre waved his hand in the air.
The threads intertwined, forming a white strand visible even to the naked eye, coiled around his wrist.
Fastening one end to the ceiling, he yanked, and his body flew up swiftly.
Shirone casted Teleportation.
However, Zenogre had already completely disappeared from sight.
He was a master beyond comprehension, at least from Shirone's perspective.
Zenogre pursued Shirone while moving along the walls.
Either way, this was a target that could be eliminated with hitman methods 90 percent of the time.
Above all, since The Last Wail of the Yeager Family no longer there, the fact that Shirone would die remained unchanged.
There was no escape for Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: However he had been hidden until now, Zenogre's murderous intent was suffocating.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Moreover, the source of the murderous intent wasn't just in one place.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: It was interfering with his Spirit Zone's synesthesia at an extraordinary speed.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: If this continues, I'll die!
In this desperate situation, Shirone gave up on fleeing.
Instead, he lowered his fist and summoned all his strength to cast Photon Explosion.
Boom, boom, boom, boom, boom!
The basement walls shook and dust fell in clumps.

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: Kekeke! Magic, eh, magic.
There is no profession that takes mages lightly, but for the assassin clan Spatur, there was always a manual for handling any situation.
As Zenogre's lips puckered, tightly coiled, spider-webbed wires shot out.
The steel wire tore right through Shirone's magic, piercing his shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Keuh!
Shirone hurriedly pulled out the wire and rolled across the floor.
It wasn't a fatal wound, but this strike made him fully aware of the gap in their skill.
Expansion-type defensive magic like Photon Explosion boasts wide-ranging defensive power, but were inevitably weak against pinpoint penetration.
A master who could see through and counter a magic's weakness the moment they saw it.
Their speed of thought likely far exceeded Shirone's.
Zenogre, who had been lingering outside Shirone's field of vision, suddenly dropped to the ground with a soundless thud.
Landing on his hands with the perfect poise of a spider.
Zenogre's tongue stretched out, licking his face from chin to brow.

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: Kiki, good reaction. I aimed for the heart but you dodged.

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: Youngsters like you are my type.

blocks.speaker: Zenogre
blocks.content: I'd like to tie you up with spider webs and 'take care of you,' but...

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: A shiver ran down Shirone's spine as he felt a chill of dread.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I'm busy myself.
As Zenogre disappeared from sight faster than before, Shirone found himself unable to judge the situation.
A mage could exert power beyond human limits, but when limited to person-to-person combat, they struggled against those who had pushed their physical abilities to the extreme.
Zenogre was a master unlike any seen before; even considering a confrontation from the start had been a grave miscalculation.
Shirone turned completely around and cast Teleportation.
If both escape and confrontation were impossible, the only remaining option was to hide and buy time.
A flash of light bounced off the walls and darted into the food storage.
As it fed hundreds of palace residents, the space was massive and sectioned by food types.
Shirone hid with his back against the pile of wheat straw that resembled a castle wall.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He hadn't cast magics for long, yet his breathing was already heavy.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Where might he target me from? The ceiling? Behind the sacks? Or the opposite section?
Shirone moved their gaze trying to find traces of Zenogre.
In reality, however, Zenogre was clinging to a corner of the storage room ceiling, completely outside Shirone's expectations.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Kikiki, how foolish. Thinking common sense applies against a master of Equalization.
All eight of Zenogre's eyes zeroed in precisely on Shirone.
Zion's combat power, borrowing the strength of Diamond Armor, matched that of a certified swordsman expert.
He was launching sharp attacks without giving Amy time to cast Teleportation.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Zion felt somewhat relieved seeing Amy's pale face.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Armand, highly valued even among S-rank Objets by standards, was indeed worth every bit.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hard work doesn't suit royalty.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Still, you're holding up quite well.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He hadn't expected the fight to last this long.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: He had thought she were a weak mage, but her movements were as agile as a swordsman's.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmph, so you prepared thoroughly since childhood? That makes it even more pitiful.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Losing to someone like me who hasn't learned even swordsmanship, let alone magic.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Stop spouting nonsense! I'll never lose to a brat like you who only relies on weapons!
Amy gritted her teeth and rushed at Zion.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She was so frustrated she felt close to tears.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: This was the result of efforts made without missing a single day for 10 years.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Someone who had never experienced hardship in their life couldn't possibly best her just by wielding a magic sword.
Amy utilized her Schema to the fullest to deploy a distraction tactic.
But Armand responded in turn, adapting Zion's physiology.
<Gothic> Enemy entity acceleration change detected. Removing retinal afterimages. Maximizing visual cognition.
The reason why afterimages occur was because when the human brain sees objects, it remembers them for one-tenth of a second.
But when Armand removed the retinal afterimage, Amy's movements became abruptly clear, as though stuttering into sharp focus.
Zion paused, then suddenly lunged.
The blade attached to his gauntlet sliced through the air, grazing the edge of Amy's collar.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: No! I absolutely can't accept this!
Amy bit down hard on her lip.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: To avoid Zion's gaze, she would need a speed that outstripped his perception.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But her Schema hadn't reached that level.
Amy entered Spirit Zone.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: In such a confined space, going up against an agile warrior equipped with close-range weapons, she wasn't sure how effective her magic would be, but if the direct approach didn't work, survival would be impossible.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Eat this!
Amy scattered Fireballs in all directions.
Instead of targeting him directly, she aimed to limit his movement—a tactical choice.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Tch! How annoying.
Zion gave up charging and retreated.
The gallery was full of various Objets.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: As long as he had Armand, the other items didn't matter, but most of them belonged to Uorin, which posed a problem.
Sensing Zion's intentions, Armand extended its tentacles in all directions.
As the tentacles clung to the walls, Zion's body lifted into the air.
The tension allowed Zion to fly like a bug.
Swinging the sword at tremendous speed, he split the Fireballs in two.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmph, you think you can block them with that?
Even if its form changes, the heat doesn't just disappear.
Just as she expected, the flames engulfed Zion, igniting the organic layer of Armand with a flash of combustion.
But as charred layers of hardened material flaked off, they were quickly replaced by new organic fibers.
Self-regeneration was a vital trait of living things.
The reason Demon Sword Armand was classified as an S-rank was due to its combination of the strengths of both metal and organic matter.
Having blocked the Fireballs, Zion looked down at Amy while standing in mid-air.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: You have unexpectedly fiery temper.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: But you won't be able to rampage any further.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Hmph, you're really going wild.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Amy knew it too—the miserable reality that the best she could was improvisation.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Moreover, the opponent's movements were unpredictable, and in this narrow space, even her specialty Sniper Mode was out of question.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: But the reason she didn't give up was because she saw a glimmer of hope.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: For some unknown reason, Zion clearly seemed to dislike the destruction of items in the room.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: They don't even look that expensive.
She had doubts, but this was the only opening in the situation.
As an experiment, Amy deliberately shot a Fire Strike toward the display case.
As expected, Zion leapt forward.
Tentacles extending from behind his shoulder blocked the Fire Strike's path.
With a bang, the tip of the tentacle began to burn.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Damn it! Block it!
As Zion's consciousness focused on the shelf, Armand withdrew its tentacles and flung its master's body backward.
Just as the display shelf was about to explode, Zion barely managed to shield himself.
As he held his breath expecting intense heat, the fireball disappointingly vanished before his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Zion
blocks.content: Wha- What the hell?
It was a fake flame, a cheap trick from Jack O'lanterns.
Since spirits possess their own independent Spirit Zone, it could be cast regardless of Amy's mental state.
